'Contemporary films about the Occupation in France sound alarm bells, with varying degrees of subtlety'
subhead: The abundance of films about World War II helps explain how the gradual abandonment of democratic and republican values led to disaster, writes historian Henry Rousso in this op-ed for Le Monde.

title: Economist Olivier Blanchard: 'France's budget process is flawed and increases the risk of a debt crisis'
subhead: The former chief economist of the International Monetary Fund argues that France must thoroughly reform the way it defines its budget. He puts forward six principles for modernizing its methods.

title: 'Sometimes I wake up in the night and I can't move': What is sleep paralysis?
subhead: This temporary phenomenon is linked to the persistence of muscle atonia associated with REM sleep. It can come with visual, auditory, or tactile hallucinations, often frightening.

'It's Mad Max on the ocean': The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
subhead: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.
byline: Liselotte Mas,  Elsa Delmas and  Thomas Saintourens

title: How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
subhead: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.

title: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subhead: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'
